item.title: Explainer: From filing to first trade: Inside the US IPO process
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX confidentially filed for what could be a record-breaking U.S. listing, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, spotlighting the multi-step process IPO hopefuls typically must navigate before their shares start trading.

item.title: Mega IPOs loom on Wall Street as Elon Musk's SpaceX confidentially files paperwork
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX has moved closer to going public with its confidential filing, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, as the billionaire's rocket-and-satellite company looks to ​beat OpenAI and Anthropic to the new listings market.

item.title: The road to SpaceX's juggernaut IPO
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX has confidentially filed for its hotly anticipated U.S. initial public offering, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, moving the billionaire closer to having a second publicly traded company in his empire.

item.title: SpaceX registers to take rocket maker public in blockbuster IPO, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX has confidentially filed for a U.S. initial public offering, setting the stage for what could become the largest stock market listing on record, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

item.title: US Gulf Coast tanker market tightens as Asia seeks to replace lost supply
item.description: Oil tanker availability along the U.S. Gulf Coast has dropped sharply in ‌recent weeks, as Asian and European refiners cut off from Middle Eastern supply have been snapping up vessels to import oil and fuel from the United States, shipping analysts and traders said.

item.title: Amazon's cloud business in Bahrain damaged in Iran strike, FT reports
item.description: Amazon's cloud computing operation in Bahrain was damaged after an Iranian strike, the ​Financial Times reported on Wednesday, citing a ‌person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iran war puts Middle East Dubai oil benchmark under stress as prices soar
item.description: The halting of oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz has left the Dubai Middle East benchmark, the price used to value nearly a fifth of global ​crude supply, in a perilous position.

item.title: Trump admin presents new plan to slash two thirds of consumer watchdog workforce
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has developed a fresh plan to slash the workforce at the U.S. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau by about two ​thirds, stepping back from earlier efforts to get rid of nearly ‌90% of all employees, court documents showed.

item.title: US business inventories unexpectedly fall in January
item.description: U.S. business inventories unexpectedly fell in January amid a large decline in stocks ​at wholesalers, suggesting inventory investment could ‌weigh on economic growth in the first quarter.

item.title: REalloys, U.S. Critical Materials sign MoU to build domestic rare earth supply chain
item.description: Rare earths developers REalloys and U.S. Critical Materials Corp have signed a memorandum of understanding ​to build a fully domestic supply chain for ‌the key materials in the U.S., in line with Washington's aims to reduce reliance on China.

item.title: Franklin Templeton to acquire CoinFund spinoff to expand crypto push
item.description: Investment manager Franklin Templeton said on Wednesday it had agreed ​to acquire cryptocurrency investment unit 250 Digital, ‌spun out of venture firm CoinFund, as it steps up its expansion into digital assets.

item.title: US manufacturing sector grows in March; supplier delivery performance deteriorates
item.description: U.S. manufacturing activity picked up in March, though a measure of prices paid by factories for inputs jumped to the highest level in nearly four years and ​suppliers took longer to deliver material amid the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Peloton wins dismissal of lawsuit claiming it concealed bike rust that heralded big recall
item.description: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit accusing Peloton Interactive of intentionally concealing quality control and safety ​problems with its exercise bikes, including allegations it told ‌factory workers to cover up rust on seat frames prior to shipping, leading to a costly recall and a declining share price.

item.title: Kia to sell lower-priced electric vehicle in US
item.description: Kia said Wednesday it ​will begin selling ‌a lower-priced electric vehicle in the United States ​later this year ​as automakers work to ⁠recharge EV sales.

item.title: McCormick bets on flavor in $65 billion Unilever tie-up amid shifting tastes
item.description: McCormick's merger with Unilever's food business to create a $65 billion sauce-and-spice giant is a bet that access to rising global demand for flavor-rich, healthier food can help counter a maturing U.S. market.

item.title: Intel to buy back Apollo stake in Ireland factory for $14.2 billion
item.description: Intel said it would spend $14.2 billion to buy back the 49% stake it sold to Apollo Global Management in ​its Ireland manufacturing facility, taking full ownership of the ‌plant as its finances improve and AI drives demand for its processors.

item.title: US retail sales increase solidly in February
item.description: U.S. retail sales increased solidly in February as motor vehicle purchases rebounded and temperatures warmed up, but surging gasoline prices because ​of war in the Middle East could crimp spending ‌in the months ahead.

item.title: Rare disease advocacy group urges Trump administration to restore FDA clarity
item.description: A rare disease advocacy coalition on Wednesday urged the ‌Trump administration to restore regulatory clarity as it considers new leadership at the U.S. Food and Drug Administration's Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research.

item.title: Hasbro investigates cybersecurity incident, takes some systems offline
item.description: Toymaker Hasbro said on Wednesday it ​was investigating a cybersecurity incident ‌after identifying unauthorized access to its network on March 28, with ​help from third-party cybersecurity ​professionals.

item.title: US private payrolls increase steadily in March, ADP says
item.description: U.S. private payrolls increased steadily in March, the ADP's national employment report ​showed on Wednesday.

item.title: India's Wipro names Nagendra Bandaru as CEO of AI segment
item.description: Indian ⁠IT ​services firm ​Wipro on ​Wednesday ​named insider Nagendra ‌Bandaru ⁠as the ​CEO ​of ⁠its ​AI ​segment.

item.title: Exclusive: Fed's Barkin says households, firms still see oil shock through a "short-term lens"
item.description: There is little evidence yet that consumers have pulled back on spending, Barkin said.

item.title: SpaceX seen as make-or-break test for mega IPOs
item.description: The global IPO market has needed a win for years and Elon Musk's SpaceX could be the breakthrough. The last company to make a market debut at over a trillion dollar valuation was Saudi Aramco in 2019.

item.title: Swiss finance minister sues for defamation over Grok-created post
item.description: Swiss Finance Minister Karin Keller-Sutter has filed a criminal complaint for defamation and insult after an X user published an obscene post about her ​created by Elon Musk's chatbot Grok, a spokesperson for her ministry ‌said on Wednesday.

item.title: Coca-Cola plans to invest $1 billion in South Africa through 2030
item.description: Coca-Cola and its two authorized bottlers, ​Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa ‌and Coca-Cola Peninsula Beverages, will invest 17.6 billion rand ($1.05 billion) ​in South Africa ​through 2030, to support expanded ⁠production capacity, the company ​said.

item.title: Exclusive: Worries over science, new addicts stymie US nicotine pouch fast-track scheme
item.description: Popular nicotine pouch products have yet to be cleared for sale in the ​United States despite a fast-track Food and Drug Administration scheme, as agency scientists hesitate to authorise them due to potential risks to new users, including ‌children, three sources told Reuters.

item.title: War-driven US dollar rebound to fade as broad safe-haven appeal erodes
item.description: The dollar's modest rebound since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran started a little over a month ago will fade as its safe-haven appeal erodes, according to currency strategists in a Reuters poll who stuck to their long-held view the ​currency will weaken.

item.title: Boeing, Lockheed Martin to boost production of PAC-3 MSE seekers, Pentagon says
item.description: The ​Pentagon on ‌Wednesday said ​it ​had reached a ⁠framework ​seven-year ​deal with Boeing Co ​and ​Lockheed Martin Corp ‌to ⁠triple production capacity ​for ​Patriot ⁠Advanced Capability-3 ​Missile ​Segment ⁠Enhancement seekers.

item.title: Former German finance minister Joerg Kukies to join Morgan Stanley
item.description: Joerg Kukies, a former German finance minister, ​will take a role at Morgan Stanley, the ‌U.S. bank said on Wednesday, marking a return to the senior ranks of ​banking after years of government ​service.

item.title: US mortgage rates jump to 6.57%, highest since August, MBA says
item.description: The interest rate on the most popular U.S. home loan jumped last week to the highest since August, as rising oil prices from ​the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran fueled inflation fears ‌and drove up yields on the Treasury bonds that lenders widely use as benchmarks for setting mortgage rates.
